panels.title: Sessions per day
panels.description: Distinct session_id per day. Filtered to $environment, excludes internal staff when $internal=false.

url_options.data: SELECT toStartOfDay(timestamp) AS day, count(DISTINCT index1) AS sessions FROM terraviz_events WHERE blob1 = 'session_start' AND blob2 = '$environment' AND blob4 = '$internal' AND timestamp > toDateTime(intDiv($__from, 1000)) GROUP BY day ORDER BY day

panels.title: Platform × OS breakdown
panels.description: Distinct sessions, grouped by platform (web/desktop/mobile) and os family.

url_options.data: SELECT blob10 AS platform, blob9 AS os, count(DISTINCT index1) AS sessions FROM terraviz_events WHERE blob1 = 'session_start' AND blob2 = '$environment' AND blob4 = '$internal' AND timestamp > toDateTime(intDiv($__from, 1000)) GROUP BY platform, os ORDER BY sessions DESC

panels.title: Layer load p95 — top 25 datasets
panels.description: p50 and p95 load time per dataset, network/HLS only (cache hits excluded).

url_options.data: SELECT blob5 AS layer_id, quantile(0.50)(double2) AS p50_ms, quantile(0.95)(double2) AS p95_ms, count() AS load_count FROM terraviz_events WHERE blob1 = 'layer_loaded' AND blob2 = '$environment' AND blob4 = '$internal' AND blob6 != 'cache' AND timestamp > toDateTime(intDiv($__from, 1000)) GROUP BY layer_id HAVING load_count > 10 ORDER BY p95_ms DESC LIMIT 25

panels.title: Load source mix
panels.description: How layers were loaded — network fetch vs local cache vs HLS vs raw image.

url_options.data: SELECT blob6 AS source, count() AS loads FROM terraviz_events WHERE blob1 = 'layer_loaded' AND blob2 = '$environment' AND blob4 = '$internal' AND timestamp > toDateTime(intDiv($__from, 1000)) GROUP BY source ORDER BY loads DESC

panels.title: Errors per session (last 24h)
panels.description: Total error events divided by distinct session count. Production-only; staff sessions excluded.

url_options.data: WITH s AS (SELECT count(DISTINCT index1) AS n FROM terraviz_events WHERE blob1 = 'session_start' AND blob2 = '$environment' AND blob4 = '$internal' AND timestamp > NOW() - INTERVAL '1' DAY), e AS (SELECT count() AS n FROM terraviz_events WHERE blob1 = 'error' AND blob2 = '$environment' AND blob4 = '$internal' AND timestamp > NOW() - INTERVAL '1' DAY) SELECT e.n / s.n AS errors_per_session FROM s, e

panels.title: Errors by category × source
panels.description: Last 7 days of error events, grouped by classified category and originating source.

url_options.data: SELECT blob5 AS category, blob8 AS source, count() AS occurrences FROM terraviz_events WHERE blob1 = 'error' AND blob2 = '$environment' AND blob4 = '$internal' AND timestamp > toDateTime(intDiv($__from, 1000)) GROUP BY category, source ORDER BY occurrences DESC

panels.title: 2D map FPS over time (median, by GPU bucket)
panels.description: Per-hour median fps from the perfSampler, split by webgl_renderer_hash. Top buckets reveal whether a GPU class is regressing.

url_options.data: SELECT toStartOfHour(timestamp) AS hour, blob6 AS gpu_bucket, quantile(0.50)(double2) AS median_fps FROM terraviz_events WHERE blob1 = 'perf_sample' AND blob5 = 'map' AND blob2 = '$environment' AND blob4 = '$internal' AND timestamp > toDateTime(intDiv($__from, 1000)) GROUP BY hour, gpu_bucket ORDER BY hour

panels.title: VR sessions — funnel + end-of-session FPS
panels.description: Per device_class × mode: count, average entry-load latency, p50 of mean_fps at session end. `device_class` and `entry_load_ms` only exist on vr_session_started; `mean_fps` only exists on vr_session_ended; we LEFT JOIN the two events on session_id so a started session that hasn't ended yet still appears with a null fps. mean_fps is the arithmetic mean over the whole session; for in-session medians query perf_sample.

url_options.data: WITH started AS (SELECT index1 AS session_id, blob5 AS device_class, blob7 AS mode, double2 AS entry_load_ms FROM terraviz_events WHERE blob1 = 'vr_session_started' AND blob2 = '$environment' AND blob4 = '$internal' AND timestamp > toDateTime(intDiv($__from, 1000))), ended AS (SELECT index1 AS session_id, double3 AS mean_fps FROM terraviz_events WHERE blob1 = 'vr_session_ended' AND blob2 = '$environment' AND blob4 = '$internal' AND double3 > 0 AND timestamp > toDateTime(intDiv($__from, 1000))) SELECT started.device_class, started.mode, count() AS sessions, avg(started.entry_load_ms) AS avg_entry_load_ms, quantile(0.50)(ended.mean_fps) AS p50_session_mean_fps FROM started LEFT JOIN ended ON started.session_id = ended.session_id GROUP BY started.device_class, started.mode ORDER BY sessions DESC

panels.title: Tour completion rates
panels.description: Started vs completed counts per tour_id; outcome='abandoned' / 'error' both excluded from completes.

url_options.data: WITH starts AS (SELECT blob6 AS tour_id, count() AS n FROM terraviz_events WHERE blob1 = 'tour_started' AND blob2 = '$environment' AND blob4 = '$internal' AND timestamp > toDateTime(intDiv($__from, 1000)) GROUP BY tour_id), completes AS (SELECT blob6 AS tour_id, count() AS n FROM terraviz_events WHERE blob1 = 'tour_ended' AND blob5 = 'completed' AND blob2 = '$environment' AND blob4 = '$internal' AND timestamp > toDateTime(intDiv($__from, 1000)) GROUP BY tour_id) SELECT starts.tour_id, starts.n AS starts, COALESCE(completes.n, 0) AS completes, COALESCE(completes.n, 0) / starts.n AS completion_rate FROM starts LEFT JOIN completes ON starts.tour_id = completes.tour_id ORDER BY starts DESC

panels.title: Country breakdown (top 20)
panels.description: Distinct sessions per country (2-letter ISO from CF-IPCountry). 'XX' (Tor / unknown) excluded.

url_options.data: SELECT blob3 AS country, count(DISTINCT index1) AS sessions FROM terraviz_events WHERE blob1 = 'session_start' AND blob2 = '$environment' AND blob4 = '$internal' AND blob3 != 'XX' AND timestamp > toDateTime(intDiv($__from, 1000)) GROUP BY country ORDER BY sessions DESC LIMIT 20

panels.title: Migration video — runs per day by outcome
panels.description: Phase 3 (catalog 3/G): per-day count of migration_r2_hls events split by outcome (ok / vimeo_fetch_failed / encode_failed / r2_upload_failed / data_ref_patch_failed). The migration is operator-driven and one-shot, so this panel is mostly empty in steady state — non-zero values during the migration window, then quiet afterwards.

url_options.data: SELECT toStartOfDay(timestamp) AS day, blob7 AS outcome, count() AS rows FROM terraviz_events WHERE blob1 = 'migration_r2_hls' AND blob2 = '$environment' AND blob4 = '$internal' AND timestamp > toDateTime(intDiv($__from, 1000)) GROUP BY day, outcome ORDER BY day, outcome

panels.title: Migration video — cumulative ok rows
panels.description: Phase 3 (catalog 3/G): total count of migration_r2_hls events with outcome='ok' in the time range. After a complete migration this should equal the legacy vimeo: row count (~136 at Phase 3 cut-over). The dashboard's time range needs to span the migration window for this number to be honest.

url_options.data: SELECT count() AS ok_rows FROM terraviz_events WHERE blob1 = 'migration_r2_hls' AND blob7 = 'ok' AND blob2 = '$environment' AND blob4 = '$internal' AND timestamp > toDateTime(intDiv($__from, 1000))

panels.title: Migration video — failure breakdown
panels.description: Phase 3 (catalog 3/G): non-ok outcome counts. Empty table after a clean migration; rows here mean the operator should re-run terraviz migrate-r2-hls and investigate the per-row stderr from that run. data_ref_patch_failed rows are the ones that leave orphan R2 prefixes — clean up via terraviz rollback-r2-hls.

url_options.data: SELECT blob7 AS outcome, count() AS rows FROM terraviz_events WHERE blob1 = 'migration_r2_hls' AND blob7 != 'ok' AND blob2 = '$environment' AND blob4 = '$internal' AND timestamp > toDateTime(intDiv($__from, 1000)) GROUP BY outcome ORDER BY rows DESC

panels.title: Migration assets — events per day by outcome
panels.description: Phase 3b (catalog 3b/J): per-day count of migration_r2_assets events split by outcome (ok / fetch_failed / upload_failed / patch_failed). One event per (row, asset_type) pair so a busy day can show 4× the row count. Mostly empty in steady state — non-zero values during the migration window, then quiet afterwards.

url_options.data: SELECT toStartOfDay(timestamp) AS day, blob8 AS outcome, count() AS rows FROM terraviz_events WHERE blob1 = 'migration_r2_assets' AND blob2 = '$environment' AND blob4 = '$internal' AND timestamp > toDateTime(intDiv($__from, 1000)) GROUP BY day, outcome ORDER BY day, outcome

panels.title: Migration assets — cumulative ok by asset_type
panels.description: Phase 3b (catalog 3b/J): total count of migration_r2_assets events with outcome='ok', grouped by asset_type. After a complete migration the four rows should land near (thumbnail: ~135, legend: ~119, caption: ~35, color_table: ~15) — the upper bounds the Phase 1d import surfaced in the SOS snapshot audit.

url_options.data: SELECT blob5 AS asset_type, count() AS ok_rows FROM terraviz_events WHERE blob1 = 'migration_r2_assets' AND blob8 = 'ok' AND blob2 = '$environment' AND blob4 = '$internal' AND timestamp > toDateTime(intDiv($__from, 1000)) GROUP BY asset_type ORDER BY ok_rows DESC

panels.title: Migration assets — failure breakdown by asset_type
panels.description: Phase 3b (catalog 3b/J): non-ok outcome counts grouped by asset_type. Empty table after a clean migration; rows here mean the operator should re-run `terraviz migrate-r2-assets` (idempotent — already-migrated assets skip) and investigate the per-asset stderr. patch_failed rows leave orphan R2 objects — clean up via `terraviz rollback-r2-assets`.

url_options.data: SELECT blob5 AS asset_type, blob8 AS outcome, count() AS rows FROM terraviz_events WHERE blob1 = 'migration_r2_assets' AND blob8 != 'ok' AND blob2 = '$environment' AND blob4 = '$internal' AND timestamp > toDateTime(intDiv($__from, 1000)) GROUP BY asset_type, outcome ORDER BY rows DESC

panels.title: Migration tours — events per day by outcome
panels.description: Phase 3c (catalog 3c/F): per-day count of migration_r2_tours events split by outcome (ok / dead_source / fetch_failed / parse_failed / sibling_fetch_failed / upload_failed / patch_failed). One event per row (tour.json + every sibling are atomic per row — distinct from migration_r2_assets's per-asset events). Mostly empty in steady state — non-zero values during the migration window, then quiet afterwards. AE positional layout: blob1=event_type, blob7=outcome (alphabetical sort of {dataset_id, legacy_id, outcome, r2_key, source_url}).

url_options.data: SELECT toStartOfDay(timestamp) AS day, blob7 AS outcome, count() AS rows FROM terraviz_events WHERE blob1 = 'migration_r2_tours' AND blob2 = '$environment' AND blob4 = '$internal' AND timestamp > toDateTime(intDiv($__from, 1000)) GROUP BY day, outcome ORDER BY day, outcome

panels.title: Migration tours — cumulative ok rows
panels.description: Phase 3c (catalog 3c/F): total count of migration_r2_tours events with outcome='ok' in the time range. After a complete Phase 3c migration this should approach the eligible-row count from the planner's dry-run summary (~198 at cut-over; 1 row — INTERNAL_SOS_726_ONLINE — has a dead NOAA source and stays on dead_source rather than ok). dead_source rows are not counted as failures and don't show here; check the per-outcome timeseries panel.

url_options.data: SELECT count() AS ok_rows FROM terraviz_events WHERE blob1 = 'migration_r2_tours' AND blob7 = 'ok' AND blob2 = '$environment' AND blob4 = '$internal' AND timestamp > toDateTime(intDiv($__from, 1000))

panels.title: Migration tours — non-ok outcome breakdown
panels.description: Phase 3c (catalog 3c/F): count of migration_r2_tours events by non-ok outcome. dead_source (NOAA 404) is informational — the row was already broken pre-migration; rerunning won't change it. fetch_failed / parse_failed / sibling_fetch_failed / upload_failed are retryable (re-run `terraviz migrate-r2-tours` — idempotent; rows already on r2: skip). patch_failed rows leave R2 orphans (the bytes uploaded, the D1 PATCH didn't); the next migrate-r2-tours run is the cheapest recovery because the uploads are idempotent — re-PUTting the same bytes to the same keys is a no-op. Alternatively `terraviz rollback-r2-tours <id>` cleans up the orphan and leaves the row on the NOAA URL.

url_options.data: SELECT blob7 AS outcome, count() AS rows FROM terraviz_events WHERE blob1 = 'migration_r2_tours' AND blob7 != 'ok' AND blob2 = '$environment' AND blob4 = '$internal' AND timestamp > toDateTime(intDiv($__from, 1000)) GROUP BY outcome ORDER BY rows DESC

panels.title: Publisher portal — loads per day by route
panels.description: Phase 3pa (catalog 3pa/F): per-day count of publisher_portal_loaded events split by route (me / datasets / tours / import / unknown). One emit per portal-chunk load — the publisher visits /publish/*, the lazy chunk resolves, the router dispatches its first route, this fires. In-portal navigation is *not* counted here; the dwell tracker (later sub-phases) covers intra-portal time-spent. Expected to be very low volume today (staff-only Phase 3); useful for the 'is anyone actually using the portal?' question once federation and Phase 3 sub-phases ship. AE positional layout: blob1=event_type, blob5=route (only field on PublisherPortalLoadedEvent so it sorts to the first user-blob slot).

url_options.data: SELECT toStartOfDay(timestamp) AS day, blob5 AS route, count() AS loads FROM terraviz_events WHERE blob1 = 'publisher_portal_loaded' AND blob2 = '$environment' AND blob4 = '$internal' AND timestamp > toDateTime(intDiv($__from, 1000)) GROUP BY day, route ORDER BY day, route

panels.title: Publisher portal — total loads
panels.description: Phase 3pa (catalog 3pa/F): total publisher_portal_loaded events in the time range. The headline 'is the portal getting used at all?' stat. A run with zero loads either means the feature is unused, the deploy hasn't shipped the portal yet, or telemetry is misconfigured — check the daily-by-route panel to disambiguate.

url_options.data: SELECT count() AS loads FROM terraviz_events WHERE blob1 = 'publisher_portal_loaded' AND blob2 = '$environment' AND blob4 = '$internal' AND timestamp > toDateTime(intDiv($__from, 1000))

panels.title: Publisher portal — loads by route
panels.description: Phase 3pa (catalog 3pa/F): publisher_portal_loaded counts grouped by the landing route. A heavy 'datasets' tilt is the expected steady-state pattern once 3pb–3pf ship (publishers spend most of their portal time on dataset CRUD). A heavy 'unknown' count flags typo'd URLs or stale bookmarks the operator might want to redirect.

url_options.data: SELECT blob5 AS route, count() AS loads FROM terraviz_events WHERE blob1 = 'publisher_portal_loaded' AND blob2 = '$environment' AND blob4 = '$internal' AND timestamp > toDateTime(intDiv($__from, 1000)) GROUP BY route ORDER BY loads DESC
